repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: finite null-annihilator / row-space criterion for proximal ATE invariance over nonunique bridge fibers

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023: proximal counterfactual means / ATE can be identified without unique bridge functions under additional regularity

repo_prior_art.cite: closest repo comparator eid_proximal_phase_v1 was a static native-objective threshold / selector-range proposal, not a local triangular-array rho_n weak-proxy phase diagram

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 gives fixed strong-functional root-n inference; AndrewsCheng2012 and AntoineLavergne2014 give general weak/semi-strong identification frameworks, not a proximal negative-control rho_n iff frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: closest repo comparator eid_proximal_phase_v1 has bridge-fiber / native-selector geometry but no rank-corrected residual projection confidence-set frontier

published_prior_art.cite: SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025 gives integral-equation confidence-set impossibility / warning results; Santos2012 and Hong2017 give NPIV or conditional-moment partial-ID inference, not this finite proximal Q_n projection statistic

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Assumption 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The target is the causal ATE E[Y(1)-Y(0)], but §2/§7 state proxy exclusions and bridge existence without explicitly stating latent exchangeability / no unmeasured confounding beyond U, so λ'β is not yet well-posed as the causal ATE.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add latent proximal exchangeability to the locked assumptions and Assumption 1, then state the bridge-to-ATE equality as the causal identification convention used by all three §8 statements.
Make Conjecture 2 explicitly say whether the residual-projection set targets the causal ATE or only the finite bridge functional when the row-space condition approaches failure.
Add a one-line comparison to Stock-Wright weak-GMM projection tests or explain why Andrews-Cheng plus Stock-Wright-Yogo already covers that axis.

recommended_next_step: Patch the missing causal exchangeability assumption and rerun Stage -0.5; the novelty kernel otherwise survives on both repo and published axes.
